claims: Plan is sound/complete/traceable with exact file ownership matching the realized diff

Fan-out policy and speed/cost semantics unchanged; state.py untouched
confidence_criteria: File ownership in plan == actual diff (PASS)

shasum/pytest re-run this gate (NOT DONE; policy-compliant self_reported)
outcome.confidence_rationale: Plan file-list matches diff exactly, all named tests exist verbatim, and source backs every step at recorded lines; plan hash corroborated by 3 prior ACCEPTed gates. Held below 0.95 because shasum and pytest were not re-run this gate (approval denied; grade=self_reported matches handoff policy).

critical_review.strongest_objection: Tests are GREEN-not-RED (implementation landed alongside the plan, so RED-first cannot be demonstrated this gate) and shasum was not re-run to re-verify the plan hash.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the declared file list diverged from the diff, a named traceability test were missing/vacuous, state.py showed a diff, or the report-only invariant were mutated, I would move to REVISE.
